step_by_step_reasoning: The transcript and whiteboard layout describe a multi-tenant healthcare data integration platform (Envoy) built on AWS. First, containerized integration engine images are stored in ECR and deployed to EKS worker nodes. Healthcare providers (UserConsumerHospital) send TCP traffic to a Network Load Balancer (NLB). The NLB forwards the traffic to an HA Proxy Ingress Controller running on EC2 instances within EKS. From there, traffic is routed to tenant-specific namespaces running on Arm-based Graviton EC2 worker nodes. Finally, customer data is persisted to dedicated, encrypted EBS GP3 volumes for storage isolation.

graph.name: Lineate Envoy Healthcare Data Integration Platform
graph.notes: Multi-tenant healthcare platform utilizing EKS with Graviton instances, NLB with HA Proxy for layer 4 TCP traffic, and dedicated EBS GP3 volumes for tenant isolation.

nodes.name: Healthcare Providers
nodes.notes: Hospitals and clinics sending healthcare data over TCP.

nodes.name: NLB
nodes.notes: Handles Layer 4 TCP traffic and whitelists source/destination IPs.

nodes.name: HA Proxy Ingress
nodes.notes: HA Proxy ingress controller running on EC2 inside the EKS cluster.

nodes.name: EKS Worker Nodes
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Arm-based Graviton instances hosting customer-segregated EKS namespaces.

nodes.name: EBS GP3
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Dedicated encrypted GP3 volumes per customer environment.

edges.notes: Deploys integration engines as container images to worker nodes.
Routes traffic securely to HA Proxy.
Writes data to isolated, encrypted EBS GP3 volumes.
